verses.text: Bezaleel, Aholiab, and every wise hearted, in whom the LORD put wisdom to work for the sanctuary, wrought according to all the LORD commanded. Moses called Bezaleel, Aholiab, and every wise hearted whose heart stirred to come unto the work. Guidance: Two qualifications: God put wisdom in their hands, and their hearts stirred to do the work. Skill without willingness leaves the gift unused; willingness without skill leaves the work unworthy. Both are gifts of God.

Bezaleel, Aholiab, and every wise hearted, in whom the LORD put wisdom to work for the sanctuary, wrought according to all the LORD commanded. Moses called Bezaleel, Aholiab, and every wise hearted whose heart stirred to come unto the work. Guidance: Two qualifications: God put wisdom in their hands, and their hearts stirred to do the work. Skill without willingness leaves the gift unused; willingness without skill leaves the work unworthy. Both are gifts of God.

They received all the offering Israel had brought. They brought yet free offerings every morning. The wise men: the people bring more than enough. Moses commanded: let no man or woman make any more for the sanctuary offering. The people were restrained. The stuff was sufficient — and too much. Guidance: The only time in Scripture God's people are told to stop giving. Generosity overflowed the project. May we ever have to be restrained from giving rather than coaxed into it.

Every wise hearted made the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen, blue, purple, scarlet, with cherubim of cunning work. Each twenty-eight by four cubits. Five coupled together, five coupled. Fifty blue loops on each set; fifty gold taches couple them — that the tabernacle might be one. Guidance: Every detail God commanded in chapter 26 now happens. The blueprint becomes weave and thread and gold. God speaks; willing wise hands do exactly what He said.

He made curtains of goats' hair for the tent over the tabernacle — eleven curtains, thirty by four cubits. Coupled five together, six together. Fifty loops, fifty brass taches to couple the tent as one. Made a covering of rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins above. Guidance: Four layers, one after another, exactly as commanded. The fanciest is innermost; the plainest outermost. God's beauty is shaped to His own design — His best is hidden, not displayed.

He made boards of shittim wood standing up — 10 by 1.5 cubits, two tenons each. Twenty south, forty silver sockets; twenty north, forty sockets. Six west, two corners — sixteen sockets. Bars of shittim wood, five each side, five west; middle bar end to end. Overlaid boards and bars with gold; gold rings. Guidance: Forty-eight boards stand exactly where God said, the middle bar runs hidden through the heart. God's design is followed to the centimeter — what holds His house must come from Him.

Made the vail of blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen — cunning work, cherubim. Four shittim pillars overlaid with gold; gold hooks; four silver sockets. Made the door hanging — blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, needlework. Five pillars with hooks; chapiters and fillets overlaid with gold; five brass sockets. Guidance: The vail comes last in this chapter, but it changes everything. Cherubim woven in — the gatekeepers of Eden again. The tabernacle is a story of regained access through atonement.
